danger_signals.visual_form: Physical health bar reduction (visual feedback during tasks like climbing or combat)
danger_signals.meaning: Player character Pino is taking damage or strain; risk of failure in current challenge

danger_signals.visual_form: Mental health bar reduction (feedback from social/cognitive tasks or being offended)
danger_signals.meaning: Pino's psychological state degraded; reduced performance in virtue circuit challenges

danger_signals.visual_form: Character animation changes when failing a challenge (implicit from animation-heavy design)
danger_signals.meaning: Insufficient character parameters (strength, luck, kindness, etc.) to succeed; must train before retry

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy or antagonist character appears in scene (confrontation sequences implied in gameplay description)
danger_signals.meaning: Challenge or conflict state; Pino must use trained parameters to succeed

safety_signals.visual_form: Sleep animation and day counter progression
safety_signals.meaning: Pino recovering health (both physical and mental); safe passage of time; minor resource cost

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible items in world (consumables like pudding, batteries, seeds, sword)
safety_signals.meaning: Training opportunity or temporary stat boost available; safety through preparation

safety_signals.visual_form: Tinker (fairy companion) visible as point-and-click interface cursor/guide
safety_signals.meaning: Guidance and support available; interface metaphor for safety through mentorship

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual feedback when circuit is activated (animation implied)
safety_signals.meaning: Pino has met challenge parameters; progression unlocked; safe advancement toward next goal

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Dr. Geppetto (authority figure), Tinker (fairy guide), Pino (protagonist)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Human civilization locations where Pino learns tasks (farming, sword combat, reading); social challenges with human NPCs

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pino (robot boy protagonist); Dr. Geppetto creates Gijin
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mechanical world elements implied by health mechanics (batteries restore physical health); 'fostering relations between humans and robot-like Gijin' is final objective

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Animation fidelity and behavioral complexity increase as Pino becomes more human; large variety of animations reflect this progression
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pino's responses to praise/scolding change; ability to perform human tasks improves; personality emerges through accumulated training

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual feedback when circuits are activated (specific visual not detailed, but implied as key state change)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pino gains ability to perform new challenges; unlocks access to new locations/tasks leading toward Circuit J activation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Day counter visible (affects certain tasks like farming and seafaring adventures)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time-gated tasks become available or progress; farming yields results; sea expeditions occur on specific day cycles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Character animations shift based on parameter levels; behavioral tells through animation variety
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Untrained Pino performs inappropriate actions requiring scolding; trained Pino exhibits desired behaviors; responses to player input reflect moral/physical development

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Environmental presentation of tasks (climbing ropes, sword fights, social interactions) with visual complexity matching Pino's current capability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Success/failure in challenges directly tied to character parameters; visual feedback through health bar changes

environmental_storytelling.example: Pino's 'very large amount of animations' communicate his journey from artificial construct to pseudo-human; animation variety itself tells the story of his development
Farming locations teach agricultural behavior; sword combat areas teach combat skill; reading areas teach intellect—world spaces encode what behaviors Pino learns there
Consumables like pudding (increases trust) and batteries (increase health) reveal the dual nature of Pino's existence (emotional and mechanical)
Success in challenges directly depends on trained parameters (strength, kindness, luck)—world responds to Pino's internal state, making stats visible through environmental interaction outcomes
Dual health bars (physical/mental) communicate that Pino is both mechanical and psychological; world state damage reflects this dual nature
Tinker (fairy companion) serves as point-and-click interface; the guide IS the interaction method, embedding narrative into UI metaphor

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Performs wrong actions requiring scolding; fails challenges; exhibits non-human behaviors; low animation variety
Performs correct actions naturally; succeeds in challenges matching his parameter levels; exhibits human-like behaviors; high animation variety and complexity
Animation shows strain; cannot perform physical tasks; reduced movement capability; must rest to recover
Animation shows distress/offense; cannot perform cognitive/social tasks; reduced task success rate; must rest or receive positive reinforcement
Engages with antagonists or difficult tasks; success depends on relevant trained parameters; animations show effort/struggle
Encourages player to replay for speed-run completion; offers secret ending access; narrative closure on human-robot relations goal
Provides point-and-click interface; present throughout as mentor/guide figure; encourages training and development

notes: Wonder Project J is a narrative-driven life simulation where world-state is primarily communicated through character animation, parameter systems, and health mechanics rather than traditional environmental design. The game's core mechanic—Pinocchio-like transformation from artificial to human—is told through behavioral animation variety and parameter-dependent task success. The dual health system (physical/mental) is a key visual metaphor for Pino's dual nature. Faction dynamics center on human-robot relations (end-goal). The game uses minimalist visual language typical of 1994 SNES era, relying heavily on animation and UI feedback to communicate world-state. Time progression (day counter) affects specific world-state changes (farming, seafaring). The interface itself (Tinker as cursor) embeds narrative relationship into gameplay mechanics.
